voices.bio_summary: English author and public educator. Author of 'This Is Vegan Propaganda' (2022) and 'How to Argue With a Meat Eater And Win Every Time' (2023). Began advocacy in March 2015 after watching the documentary Earthlings. Approach is calm, Socratic, evidence-based. Core argument: the capacity to suffer is the only relevant criterion for moral consideration.

voices.lens: Compute acceleration, technological progress as moral imperative
voices.bio_summary: Co-founder and CEO of NVIDIA, the company whose GPUs power most modern AI training and inference. Believes massively scalable compute is the most transformative lever in human history. Known for radically flat organizational structure (~60 direct reports, no 1:1s, public feedback only). Frames technological progress as a moral imperative; views constraints on compute or energy as constraints on human potential.

voices.lens: Contrarian zero-to-one innovation, anti-democracy, sovereign individual
voices.bio_summary: Co-founder of PayPal and Palantir, early Facebook investor, author of 'Zero to One' (2014). Famously stated he no longer believes freedom and democracy are compatible. Stagnation Thesis: the physical world has stagnated while the digital world has accelerated. Sovereign Individual framing: visionary individuals shape history. Singularitarian — interest in life extension and AI to transcend biological limits.
voices.note: Not yet scored in voices.json canonical roster — v02 was first appearance.
